question: Which hydro-electric project produces maximum power energy? (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: Bhakra Nangal
option_2: Koyna
option_3: Hirakud
option_4: Salal
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (B) The Koyna Hydroelectric Project is the second largest hydroelectric power plant in India, just after the Tehri Dam Project. This 1,960 MW plant is the largest completed hydroelectric power plant in India. It is a complex project with four dams including the largest dam on the Koyna River, Maharashtra, hence the name Koyna Hydroelectric Project, Which is located in Satara district. The Bhakra Nangal Dam is located on the Sutlej River in Himachal Pradesh, India. The dam is the second highest in Asia, standing at about 207 meters tall. It was constructed by the Indian Army and opened to the public in 1975. The Hirakud Dam is built across the Mahanadi River in Odisha, India.The Hirakud Dam is the longest earthen dam in the world and the longest major earthen dam in Asia. What is an earthen dam? An earthen dam, also known as an earth dam, is made primarily of soil and rock materials, such as clay, silt, sand, and gravel. Earthen dams typically store water or create a barrier to control or divert water flow. The Salal Dam, also known as the Salal Hydroelectric Power Station, is located on the Chenab River in the Reasi district of Jammu and Kashmir.

question: Consumer welfare is indicated by.......... (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: Savings
option_2: Disposable income
option_3: Expenditure
option_4: None of the above
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (D) Consumer welfare refers to the well-being and satisfaction of consumers within a market. It encompasses various factors such as access to quality goods and services, fair pricing, product safety, and the ability of consumers to make informed choices. The Consumer Protection Act of 1986 in India protects consumers from fraud and unfair practices. The act also encourages consumers to speak out against flaws in goods and services.

question: Productivity being low, cottage industry produced a lot because............ (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: It employs large number of people
option_2: No power is needed to manufacture most of the products
option_3: Raw material is generally available locally
option_4: It caters mostly to the rural population
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (A) A cottage industry is a small manufacturing business that is owned and operated by an individual or a family, typically operating out of a home rather than a purpose-built facility. Cottage industries can produce a lot despite low productivity because they employ a large number of people. Few Example of Cottage industry are Cotton weaving, Carpet Weaving, Silk Weaving , Basket Weaving , Pottery figurines, etc

question: What is the most remarkable aspect of Indian industry since independence? (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: More employment opportunities
option_2: Increase in production
option_3: Capacity utilisation
option_4: Diversification
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (D) The most remarkable aspect of Indian industry since independence is its diversification. India has moved from an agrarian economy to a service economy, with the services sector now dominating GDP. The sectors that have experienced the most growth are services and capital-intensive manufacturing. Other notable achievements of the Indian manufacturing industry include: 1) Self-reliance 2) Growth 3) IT hub 4) Exports

question: What is Government’s trade policy? (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: Export promotion
option_2: Import substitution
option_3: Both of the above
option_4: None of the above
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (C) A government’s trade policy is a set of laws, regulations, and agreements that govern a country’s imports and exports. Trade policies aim to strengthen the domestic economy. In which There is Promotion Of Export And Substitution of Imports. Trade policies can include: 1) Taxes on inspection regulations 2) Import and export 3) Tariffs and quotas 4) Protection of domestic manufacturers from foreign competition

question: Which two of the following are animal products? (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: Leather and meat
option_2: Silk and polyester
option_3: Linen and silk
option_4: Cotton and silk
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (A) An animal product is any material derived from the body of a non-human animal. Leather is a strong, flexible and durable material obtained from the tanning, or chemical treatment, of animal skins and hides to prevent decay. The most common leathers come from cattle, sheep, goats, equine animals, buffalo, pigs and hogs, and aquatic animals such as seals and alligators. Tamil Nadu is the largest leather producing state in India, accounting for 40% of the country’s leather production. Kanpur city is the largest center of leather industry in the country. India is the 4th largest exporter of leather goods in the world.

question: “Gresham’s Law” in Economics relates to............. (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: Supply and demand
option_2: Circulation of currency
option_3: Consumption and supply
option_4: Distribution of goods and services
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (B) Gresham’s law is an economic principle that states that “bad money drives out good”. It relates to the circulation of currency. Bad money : It is a currency with equal or less value than its face value. Good money : It has the potential for a greater value than its face value.

question: Cause of inflation is............ (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: Increase in money supply
option_2: Fall in production
option_3: Increase in money supply and fall in production
option_4: Decrease in money supply and fall in production
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (C) Inflation is a gradual increase in the prices of goods and services. It can be caused by a number of factors, including:1) An increase in the money supply can lead to inflation. Monetary authorities can increase a country’s money supply by printing and distributing more money to citizens. 2) When demand exceeds supply, prices rise. There are mainly two types of inflatons : 1) Demand-Pull Inflation: Demand pull inflation occurs when the demand for goods and services in an economy exceeds the supply.It’s often described as “too much money chasing too few goods”. 2) Cost Push Inflation : Cost-push inflation is a type of inflation that occurs when the cost of production increases, leading to higher prices for goods and services. It’s also known as wage-push inflation.

question: The Government’s gold auction policy is aimed at............ (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: Checking of smuggling and reducing the budgetary deficit of the Central Government
option_2: Promoting jewellery exports
option_3: Making gold available to consumers for ornaments.
option_4: Price stabilization
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (A) The government’s gold auction policy aims to reduce the budgetary deficit of the Central Government and check smuggling. Bank auctions for gold can provide buyers with an opportunity to purchase gold at a discounted price.  Bank auctions also offer a level of transparency and security that can be difficult to find elsewhere in the gold market.  Gold auctions can have several benefits, including : Discounts, Transparency and security, Easy access to funds, etc

question: The countries of the world are economically interdependent. This fact is proved............ (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: By turmoil in Iran
option_2: By hike in oil prices by OPEC and rise in taxi fares all over
option_3: Because India has political relations with almost all countries of the world
option_4: Because multinationals have set up a number of projects in India
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (B) Correct option is B. By hike in oil prices by OPEC and rise in taxi fares all over. Economic interdependence is a situation where two or more parties depend on each other to exchange goods and fulfill their needs. Some factors that lead to economic interdependence include : Industrialization, Economic advancement, Labor specialization, Regional production. Both advanced and less developed economic nations experience both positive and negative effects from economic interdependence. What is OPEC? The Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) is an intergovernmental organization that coordinates petroleum policies among its member countries. OPEC’s goal is to ensure stable prices for petroleum producers, a reliable supply of petroleum to consuming nations, and a fair return on capital for investors in the industry. OPEC was founded in 1960 by Iran, Iraq, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, and Venezuela. Venezuela is the OPEC member state with the largest crude oil reserves, in 2022. India is not a permanent member of OPEC. However, India and OPEC regularly hold Energy Dialogue meetings.

question: The per capita income of India in 1977-78 at 1970-71 prices was............ (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: Rs. 590
option_2: Rs. 690
option_3: Rs. 790
option_4: Rs. 890
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (B) The per capita income of India in 1977-78 at 1970-71 prices was ₹690. What is Per Capita Income? Per capita income (PCI), measures the average income earned per person in a given area (city, region, country, etc) in a specified year. It is calculated by dividing the area’s total income by its total population. Per capita income is national income divided by population size. Per capita income is also often used to measure a country’s standard of living.

question: In the first half of the year 1978, the relations between India and U.S.A. suffered setback on account of.......... (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: US declining for the supply of uranium for Tarapur atomic power plant
option_2: US pressurising India to sign the Non- Proliferation Treaty
option_3: Banning the consumption of Coca Cola in the country
option_4: All factors listed above
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (D) All given factors were responsible behind strained relationship between India and USA in 1978. The Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons (NPT) is a multilateral treaty that aims to limit the spread of nuclear weapons. The treaty has three elements: 1. Non-proliferation, 2. disarmament, and 3. peaceful use of nuclear energyIndia is one of the countries that either did not sign the NPT or signed but withdrew. South Sudan, India, Pakistan, Israel have not signed the Non-Proliferation Treaty (NPT) India considered the NPT as discriminatory and had refused to sign it.

question: Temporary control of inflation can be effected by........... (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: Increasing the prices
option_2: Increasing the taxes
option_3: Restraint on the growth
option_4: Restraint on the growth
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (B) Inflation is a general increase in the prices of goods and services in an economy. It’s usually measured using the consumer price index (CPI). Inflation can be controlled by a contractionary monetary policy. This policy aims to reduce the supply of money within an economy by lowering the prices of bonds and rising interest rates. Monetary policy primarily involves changing interest rates to control inflation. Governments can reduce spending and increase taxes as a way to help Control inflation.

question: Sudden decrease of birth rate would cause............. (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: Increase in investment
option_2: Increase of savings
option_3: Increase in per capita income
option_4: Increase in production
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (C) Birth rate is the ratio of the number of live births in a year to the average total population of that year. A sudden decrease in birth rate can cause a number of issues, including: 1) A sudden decrease in birth rate can lead to an increase in per capita income.2) A smaller workforce can mean less money for social safety nets. 3) A smaller workforce can lead to labor shortages, skill gaps, and reduced innovation.

question: India spent most of its foreign exchange reserves in 1977-78 for............ (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: Import of chemicals and fertilisers
option_2: Import of crude petroleum
option_3: Import of newsprint
option_4: Combating inflation
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (B) India spends the majority of its foreign exchange on oil and petroleum products. India spends around 75% of its export earnings on crude oil. India’s foreign exchange reserves are made up of major currencies like the US Dollar, the Euro, and the British Pound. These currencies provide liquidity and allow the country to pay for international trade transactions. During 1977-78, due to heavy industrialization going in india and to cater the Industrialization needs India had to import Crude Oil in large Scale.

question: Buyers market denotes the place where............ (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: The demand exceeds the supply
option_2: The supply exceeds the demand
option_3: The demand and supply are well balanced
option_4: Commodities are available at competitive rates
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (B) A buyer’s market is a situation where supply exceeds demand. In this type of market, buyers have the advantage of price negotiation. A seller’s market is a marketplace where there are fewer goods for sale than there are interested buyers. This gives the seller the ability to dictate prices.

question: India earns maximum foreign exchange by the export of which of the following commodities? (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: Iron
option_2: Tea
option_3: Jute
option_4: Sugar
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (B) The Tea Industry in India is the major foreign exchange earner and labour intensive employment generation sector. India is the second largest producer of tea in the world after China. In 2021–2022, India exported $2.5 billion worth of tea, which accounted for 12.5% of the country’s total agricultural exports. India also earns foreign exchange from the export of gems and jewelry. India is a leading exporter of gems and jewelry, including diamonds, precious stones, gold, silver, and platinum. India has a large supply of raw materials, a skilled workforce, and low labor costs. What is foreign exchange? Foreign exchange (forex or FX) is the conversion of one country’s currency into another.  The conversion rate is known as the foreign exchange rate.

question: Which of the following fertiliser plants is not in the public sector? (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: Gorakhpur
option_2: Nangal
option_3: Kota
option_4: Bhatinda
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (C) The Kota fertilizer plant not comes under the public sector. Chambal Fertilisers and Chemicals Limited, one of India’s largest private fertilizer producers, was founded in 1985 by Zuari Industries Limited in Kota, Rajasthan.What Are Fertilisers? Fertilizers are natural or artificial substances that contain chemical elements that improve plant growth and productivity. These substances include nitrogen (N), phosphorus (P), and potassium (K). Fertilizers are also known as “enrichment” or “plant nutrient”. Fertilizers improves the soil’s fertility or replace the chemical ingredients removed by previous crops. What is Manure? Manure is organic matter that is used as organic fertilizer in agriculture. Most manure consists of animal feces; other sources include compost and green manure. Manures contribute to the fertility of soil by adding organic matter and nutrients

question: Which among the following States depends primarily on thermal power? (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: Kerala
option_2: West Bengal
option_3: Tamil Nadu
option_4: Karnataka
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (B) The Indian state of West Bengal primarily depends on thermal power. Thermal power generation consists of using steam power created by burning oil, liquid natural gas (LNG), coal, and other substances to rotate generators and create electricity.

question: Maximum number of workers are employed in which of the following industries in India? (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: Sugar
option_2: Textiles
option_3: Jute
option_4: Iron and Steel
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (B) The textile industry continues to be the second-largest employment generating sector in India, after agriculture.The textile industry is primarily concerned with the design, production and distribution of textiles: yarn, cloth and clothing. Indian textile industry employs over 45 million people directly and another 60 million people indirectly. Textile industry comes under Ministry Of Textiles.

question: The construction of Marmugao port has been fast in recent years because of........... (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: Increased Imports
option_2: Exporting iron ore from hinterland
option_3: Decreasing the congestion caused due to heavy traffic at the Bombay port
option_4: All factors listed above
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (D) All the factors Above Listed are Responsible for Construction of Marmugao port. Mormugao is a seaport city situated in the Mormugao taluka (municipality) of the South district, in the Goa state. It has a deep natural harbour and remains Goa’s chief port. Natural harbors are places where boats can come from a larger body of water and have shelter from the winds. Ever since it was accorded the status of a Major Port in 1963, the Mormugao port has contributed immensely to growth of maritime trade in India. The INS Mormugao has been named after the port.

question: Underground Railways are being built in India at............ (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: Bombay
option_2: Calcutta
option_3: Hyderabad
option_4: Madras
solution_text:
EXPLANATION : (B)

The first underground railway in India was constructed in Calcutta (now Kolkata). The Kolkata Metro, which began construction in the 1970s, became operational in 1984, making it not only the first metro railway in India but also the first underground railway system in the country. The project was undertaken to reduce traffic congestion and improve public transportation in the densely populated city.

question: “Socialistic Pattern” comes through............ (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: Free Economy
option_2: Mixed Economy
option_3: Public Sector
option_4: None of these
solution_text: EXPLANATION : ( C ) Public Sector is dominant in Socialist pattern economy. According to the Planning Commission, a socialist pattern of society is when social gain is the main criterion for determining than private profit. It also involves planning the structure of socio-economic relations and the pattern of development. There is direct public ownership, wherein production is carried out directly for use.

question: Allocation for power sector during the 6th Five-Year Plan (1978-83) is............... (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: 23%
option_2: 28%
option_3: 33%
option_4: 38%
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (A) The Sixth Five-Year Plan was led by Indira Gandhi and marked the beginning of economic liberalization in India. The plan eliminated price controls and closed ration shops, which led to higher food prices and the cost of living. Allocation has been devoted to a rapid increase in India’s electrical capacity. Emphasis was placed on increasing national income, modernizing technology, and ensuring a continuous decrease in poverty and unemployment. Adopted with the slogan ‘Garibi Hatao’. There was a shift from industrialization toward infrastructure development, with programs such as the National Rural Employment Programme, Integrated Rural Development Programme, and Village and Small Industries Development Programme focusing on employment generation.

question: China intends to purchase from U.K............. (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: Hunters
option_2: Harriers
option_3: Sabers
option_4: Jaguars
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (B) The Correct Ans is B China Purchase Harriers From UK

question: What is a Scheduled Bank? (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: A bank having Rs. 1000 crore deposits.
option_2: A bank included in the Second Schedule of the RBI Act
option_3: A bank having Rs. 10 crore deposits
option_4: A bank having Rs. 100 crore deposits
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (B) A scheduled bank is a bank that is listed in the second schedule of the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, and fall under the regulatory framework of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949. To qualify as a scheduled bank, a bank’s paid-up capital and raised funds must be at least Rs:5 lakh. The Regional Rural Banks and Scheduled Commercial banks support SHGs. Some of the main functions of scheduled banks include: 1 Accepting deposits from the public. 2. Providing demand withdrawal facilities. 3. Lending facilities. 4. Transferring funds. 5. Issuing drafts. 6. Providing customers with locker facilities. 7. Dealing with foreign exchange. There are two main categories of scheduled banks in India, namely: 1. Scheduled Commercial banks 2. Scheduled Co-operative banksScheduled commercial Banks are further divided into six types, as below: i) Scheduled Public Sector Banks ii) Scheduled Private Sector Banks iii) Scheduled Small Finance Banks iv) Regional Rural Banks v) Foreign Banks vi) Payment banks (currently four banks Airtel Payments Bank, Fino Payments Bank, India Post Payments Bank, Paytm Payments Bank have been granted Scheduled bank status). Payment Banks : Established on recommendations of Usha Thorat Committee; Licensed under Banking Regulation Act, 1949. FDI is allowed in payment banks.They cannot set up subsidiaries to undertake non-banking activities. Scheduled Co-operative banks are further divided into 2 types namely: 1. Scheduled State Co-operative banks. 2. Scheduled Urban cooperative banks.

question: A bank having Rs. 100 crore deposits............ (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: Tamil Nadu
option_2: Assam
option_3: Kerala
option_4: Punjab
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (C) The Correct Ans is C The cashew tree (Anacardium occidentale) is a tropical evergreen tree native to South America. The tree can grow as tall as 14 metres (46 feet). Red laterite soil is the most suitable soil for growing cashew nuts. These soils are rich in iron and aluminum and are formed by the weathering of laterite rocks. Cashew is a tropical plant that can thrive in high temperatures. It can also grow in poor soils, but performs better in good soils. Cashew plants can adapt to any climate without affecting productivity.Kollam city of Kerela is known as the Cashew Capital of the World as Kollam is the largest producer of Cashew in the world. There are many cashew processing units present in the city. Around 80 per cent of India’s export quality, cashew nuts get prepared in Kollam.

question: What is the likely impact of decrease of population on economy? (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: Increase in per capita income
option_2: Decrease in per capita income
option_3: Increase in investment
option_4: Decrease in savings
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (A) The Correct Ans is A What is Per-Capita Income? Per capita income is a measure of the average income earned per person in a specific geographic area, such as a country or region. It is calculated by dividing the total income of a country by its population. Any change in population affect the per capita income. For example population increases per capita income decreases; if population decreases per capita increases.

question: Unemployment insurance will result in............ (UPSC Prelims 1979)
option_1: Maintenance of consumption level
option_2: Decrease in consumption level
option_3: Decrease in savings
option_4: Increase in savings
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (A) The Correct Ans is A Unemployment insurance is a state-run program that provides financial assistance to unemployed individuals who meet certain eligibility requirements. These benefits are not available to workers who voluntarily quit or were fired from a previous job.Due to unemployment insurance, people are insured for their maintenance of consumption level. This Unemployment Insurance is a government scheme

question: Which statement about Colombo Plan is incorrect? (UPSC Prelims 1980)
option_1: The headquarters of the Colombo Plan are at Colombo
option_2: This is a plan of economic development for South and South East Asian countries
option_3: It is going to expire in 1981.
option_4: The Colombo Plan celebrated its Silver Jubilee in 1976
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (C) The Colombo Plan is a regional intergovernmental organization that fosters economic and social development in the Asia-Pacific region. It was established in 1951. Its scope has expanded to include various areas such as human resources development, trade, and investment. Member countries collaborate through the Colombo Plan to promote regional cooperation and development initiatives.

question: Oil has been struck in which of the following places recently by the ONGC? (UPSC Prelims 1980)
option_1: Gunupur
option_2: Belonia
option_3: Ankleshwar
option_4: Krishna Godavari basin
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (D) The Correct option is D - Krishna Godavari basin What is ONGC? ONGC stands for Oil and Natural Gas Corporation Limited. It is an Indian multinational oil and gas company, engaged in exploration,production, and refining of petroleum. Krishna Godavari Basin : The Krishna Godavari Basin is one of the largest sedimentary basins in India, located on the eastern coast. It is known for its significant reserves of oil and natural gas. The basin has gained prominence due to exploration and production activities by companies like ONGC (Oil and Natural Gas Corporation Limited) and Reliance Industries. Gunupur is a town in the Rayagada district of Odisha, India. It is the second largest town in the district and is located on the banks of the Vamsadhara River in the Eastern Ghats region. Ankleshwar is a city in the Bharuch district of Gujarat, India. It is known for its petrochemical industry and is home to Gujarat’s largest oil field.

question: What was the impact of Western Industrial Revolution on India? (UPSC Prelims 1980)
option_1: Handicrafts of India were ruined
option_2: Machines were introduced in textile industry
option_3: Heavy import duty was imposed on foreign goods
option_4: All technical hands got employment
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (A) The Western Industrial Revolution, which began in the late 18th century, had profound and far-reaching impacts on various aspects of society, economy, and technology. Some key impacts include: 1) Handicrafts Of India Ruined. 2) Growth of industries resulted in the migration of people from rural areas to urban in search of Employment. 3) Technological innovations, including the steam engine, mechanized textile production,etc. 4) Industrial Revolution ultimately led to improved standards of living for many people.

question: Highest per capita income is in............ (UPSC Prelims 1980)
option_1: Punjab
option_2: West Bengal
option_3: Tamil Nadu
option_4: Gujarat
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (A) The Correct option is A. Punjab In 2022-2023, Punjab’s per capita income was Rs 1,73,873, which is higher than the national average of Rs 1,70,620. This is a 7% increase from the previous year. Note : Currently,According to the Ministry of Statistics & Programme Implementation (MoSPI), Telangana has the highest per capita income in India at Rs 3,08,732 in 2022-23.

question: Rolling Plan can be best defined in context of India as.......... (UPSC Prelims 1980)
option_1: Formulation of annual plans
option_2: Perspective of Five-Year Plan with the provision of extending by one year at a time so that there would be a constant planning horizon of five years
option_3: Aims and achievements reviewed every year in a Five-Year Plan
option_4: Plan for full 5 years
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (B) A rolling plan is an economic planning concept where the plan is continuously updated or extended for a specific period, typically five years. Instead of having fixed plans for a set number of years, a rolling plan allows for periodic adjustments based on changing circumstances and requirements. For instance, the Eighth Five-Year Plan (1992-1997) and subsequent plans were designed as rolling plans. This approach provides flexibility in adapting to evolving economic conditions and priorities without being bound to a rigid long-term plan.

question: Antyodaya scheme is meant............ (UPSC Prelims 1980)
option_1: To help the Muslims
option_2: To help the Harijans
option_3: To help the minorities.
option_4: To help the economically weakest sections of the society
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (D) The Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) is a social welfare program in India aimed at providing highly subsidized food to the poorest of the poor families. Launched by the Government of India, this scheme falls under the broader umbrella of the Public Distribution System (PDS). Under Antyodaya Anna Yojana, eligible households receive food grains at very low prices to ensure food security for those who are economically vulnerable. Each family enrolled in the AAY Scheme is eligible to receive up to 35 kg of food grains every month. The food grains are provided at a subsidized price of Rs. 3 per kilogram for rice and Rs. 2 per kilogram for wheat. It is one of the initiatives to address the issue of helping the economically Weakest Section of The Society.

question: Hong Kong is.............. (UPSC Prelims 1980)
option_1: A free port
option_2: An independent state
option_3: A free exchange market
option_4: All of the above
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (C) The Correct option is C. A free exchange market Hong Kong is a Special Administrative Region (SAR) of China. It was a British colony until 1997 when it was handed back to China under the principle of “one country, two systems.” Hongkong has free market economy, Hongkong is highly dependent on International Trade.

question: The only State in India that shows excess of females over males is............ (UPSC Prelims 1980)
option_1: U.P.
option_2: Kerala
option_3: Maharashtra
option_4: Tamil Nadu
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (B) The state in India that showed an excess of females over males was Kerala. Kerala has a unique demographic profile with a higher female-to-male ratio compared to many other states in India. According to the 2011 census, Kerala has a sex ratio of 1084 females for every 1,000 males.

question: Mixed economy refers to............ (UPSC Prelims 1980)
option_1: The coexistence of heavy, small scale and cottage industries
option_2: The promotion of agriculture as well as cottage industries
option_3: The coexistence of rich as well as poor
option_4: Co-existence of public as well as private sector
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (D) Mixed Economy is The Combination of both Capitalism and Socialism A mixed economy is an economic system that incorporates elements of both market and planned economies. In a mixed economy, the government and the private sector coexist, with each playing a role in economic decision-making. The government typically intervenes to regulate certain industries, provide public goods and services, and address market failures, while the private sector operates within a framework of market forces. Many modern economies, including India, United States and many European countries, are considered mixed economies.

question: Whose signature is found on a hundred rupee note? (UPSC Prelims 1980)
option_1: President of India
option_2: Governor of Reserve Bank of India
option_3: Prime Minister
option_4: Finance Minister
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (B) Only The One Rupee note is issued by Ministry of Finance and Which is Signed by Finance Secretary, whereas other Currency notes are signed by the RBI Governor

question: Qantas Airways belongs to........... (UPSC Prelims 1980)
option_1: Australia
option_2: West Germany
option_3: East Germany
option_4: New Zealand
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (A) Qantas is the flag carrier airline of Australia, and it’s one of the world’s oldest and most reputable airlines. Established in 1920, Qantas stands for “Queensland and Northern Territory Aerial Services.” It operates both domestic and international flights and has a strong reputation for safety and reliability.

question: The Bhilai Steel Plant was set up with the collaboration of the U.S.S.R., the Rourkela Steel Plant with the collaboration of West Germany, the Durgapur Steel Plant was constructed with the collaboration of.............. (UPSC Prelims 1980)
option_1: Britain
option_2: Japan
option_3: U.S.A.
option_4: Romania
solution_text:
EXPLANATION : (A) 

According to India’s industrial history, during the Second Five-Year Plan, three major public sector steel plants were established with foreign assistance: Bhilai Steel Plant — with U.S.S.R. question: To ensure the efficiency, economic condition; etc. there are Control Boards for each major project except one for which a statutory corporation has been set up. That project is.............. (UPSC Prelims 1980)

question: Farakka agreement between Bangladesh and India implies............ (UPSC Prelims 1980)

A riparian state is a state that is located along the border or through the territory of a watercourse.

question: The average rate of increase of per capita income in 1978-79 was........... (UPSC Prelims 1981)

question: What was the main aim of Bearer Bonds Scheme? (UPSC Prelims 1981)

question: Which of the following is incorrect? (UPSC Prelims 1981)
option_1: 1951-56 First Five Year Plan
option_2: 1956-61 Second Five Year Plan
option_3: 961-66 Third Five Year Plan
option_4: 1966-71 Fourth Five Year Plan
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (D) The First Five-Year Plan in India covered the period from 1951 to 1956. It was launched in an effort to kickstart economicdevelopment in the newly independent country. The primary focus of the plan was on agriculture, with an emphasis on increasing food production and addressing famine-like situations. The plan also aimed to develop basic industries and infrastructure. The First Five-Year Plan laid the foundation for subsequent plans and played a crucial role in shaping India’s economic policies. The Second Five-Year Plan in India spanned from 1956 to 1961. It continued the focus on economic development initiated by the First Five-Year Plan. The main objectives of the Second Plan included rapid industrialization, particularly in heavy industries, and the establishment of a self-reliant economy. This plan also aimed at reducing disparities in income and standard of living among different sections of society. The Second Five-Year Plan set the stage for further industrialization and economic growth in India. The Third Five-Year Plan in India covered the period from 1961 to 1966. It aimed at achieving self-sufficiency in food production, emphasizing agriculture and related sectors. The plan also focused on strengthening the industrial base of the country, particularly in areas of machine building and capital goods production. The Fourth Five-Year Plan in India spanned from 1969 to 1974. It was formulated with the aim of achieving self-sufficiency in food production and addressing poverty and unemployment. This plan emphasized agriculture and industry, and it also focused on improving social services like education and healthcare.

question: Mixed economy means........... (UPSC Prelims 1981)

The government typically intervenes to regulate certain industries, provide public goods and services, and address market failures, while the private sector operates within a framework of market forces. Many modern economies, including India,United States and many European countries, are considered mixed economies.

question: The recent IMF loan to India will help immediately.............. (UPSC Prelims 1982)

solution_text: EXPLANATION : (C) The correct ans is Option C. To correct the Adverse Balance of Payments. The International Monetary Fund (IMF) was established in 1944 and came into operation on December 27, 1945. The IMF’s formation was initiated at the Bretton Woods Conference in July 1944. The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has 190 member countries. India is a member of the International Monetary Fund (IMF). India joined the IMF on December 27, 1945, and has not received any financial assistance from the IMF since 1993. The IMF’s intellectual founding fathers are John Maynard Keynes and Harry Dexter White.The IMF was founded by 44 member countries, including India, which sought to create a framework for economic cooperation with the newest member, Andorra joining the IMF in October 2020. The International Monetary Fund (IMF) is an international financial institution established to foster global monetary cooperation, secure financial stability, facilitate international trade, promote high employment and sustainable economic growth, and reduce poverty around the world. It provides financial assistance to member countries facing balance of payments problems and offers policy advice, technical assistance, and economic research. What is Balance of Payment? The balance of payments is a record of a country’s economic transactions with the rest of the world, including trade, investments, and financial flows. It consists of the current account (trade in goods and services) and the capital and financial account (financial transactions). If a country exports more than it imports, it has a surplus; otherwise, it has a deficit.

question: First Five-Year Plan laid stress on........... (UPSC Prelims 1982)
option_1: Agriculture
option_2: Industry
option_3: Power
option_4: Uplifting of poor people
solution_text:
EXPLANATION : (A) 

India’s First Five-Year Plan (1951–1956) was the country’s first systematic effort at planned economic development after independence. It was launched under Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru, but the main architect behind its formulation was Planning Commission member K.N. Raj, who emphasized the need to stabilize the economy before pursuing rapid industrialization. At the time of independence, India faced severe food shortages, low agricultural productivity, and inadequate irrigation infrastructure. The partition had also reduced agricultural land and created food insecurity. Therefore, the First Plan gave highest priority to agriculture, irrigation, and rural development — to ensure food self-sufficiency and to lay the foundation for future industrial growth. The plan adopted the Harrod–Domar growth model, focusing on increasing investment and capital formation. About 44% of the total plan outlay was allocated to agriculture and community development programmes, including projects like the Bhakra-Nangal Dam, Hirakud Dam, and Damodar Valley Project, which were aimed at expanding irrigation and power supply. By the end of the plan period (1956), agricultural output had increased significantly, and India achieved notable progress in food grain production and rural infrastructure. Although other sectors like power and transport were developed to support agriculture, the central focus of the First Five-Year Plan remained agriculture and rural upliftment. The First Five-Year Plan (1951–56) mainly stressed agriculture to ensure food security, improve irrigation, and strengthen the rural economy — laying the groundwork for India’s later industrial development.

question: Which tax is not levied by the Central Government? (UPSC Prelims 1982)

solution_text: EXPLANATION : (D) The correct answer is Excise Tax. Excise tax is not levied by the central Government. What is Wealth Tax? In these industries, a significant portion of the production process is automated or relies heavily on advanced technology. The goal is often to increase efficiency, improve output quality, and reduce long term production costs,Which Gives Blow to the growth of large scale industries. Few examples of capital-intensive industries are :1) Automobile Manufacturing 2) Chemical Manufacturing 3) Steel and Metal Production 4) Oil Refining 5) Aerospace and Defense 6) Telecommunications 7) Semiconductor Manufacturing 8) Automated Manufacturing

question: Melbourne declaration was connected with............ (UPSC Prelims 1982)
option_1: Environmental pollution
option_2: Energy crisis
option_3: Reducing the economic inequality between the developed and the developing countries
option_4: Human rights
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (C) What is Melbourne Declaration? The Melbourne Declaration of Educational Goals for Young Australians, issued by all Australian Ministers for Education in December 2008. The Melbourne Declaration serves as a guiding document for educational policy and practice in Australia, influencing curriculum development, teachingstrategies, and assessment methods to align with the stated goals. Melbourne declaration is concerned with reducing the economic inequality between the developed and the developing countries. It reflects a commitment to providing a high-quality education that prepares young Australians for the challenges and opportunities of the 21st century.

question: Nehru Model of Development laid greater stress on........... (UPSC Prelims 1982)
option_1: Collective use of land
option_2: Using imported seeds
option_3: Agricultural development
option_4: Rapid industrialisation with particular emphasis on the development of basic and heavy industries
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (D) The “Nehru Model of Development” refers to the economic policies and development strategies implemented by Jawaharlal Nehru, the first Prime Minister of India, during his tenure from 1947 to 1964. Nehru’s vision for India’s development was influenced by socialist ideals and the need to address the socio-economic challenges faced by the newly independent nation. Nehru Model of Development Stressed Mainly on Rapid Industrialisation with Particular Emphasis on the development of basic and heavy Industries.

question: The death rate according to 1981 census is per thousand persons............. (UPSC Prelims 1983)
option_1: 14.8
option_2: 15.8
option_3: 10.8
option_4: 9.8
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (D) According to the 1981 census of India, the national death rate was approx 9.8 per 1,000. According to the 2011 Census of India, the crude death rate was 7.1 per thousandpopulation and the birth rate in India is 21.8 infants born per 1,000 people each year. The total fertility rate (TFR) in India is between 2.1 and 2.9. The 2011 census also found that the lowest birth rate was in rural areas of Kerala (14.3) and in urban areas of Himachal Pradesh (10.5).

question:
The correct sequence of the following densely populated States is............ (UPSC Prelims 1983)

1. Uttar Pradesh

2. Kerala

3. Bihar

4. West Bengal

Select the correct answer from the codes given below:
option_1: 2, 3, 1, 4
option_2: 4, 1, 2, 3
option_3: 1, 4, 2, 3
option_4: 2, 4, 3, 1
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (B) The correct sequence of the following densely populated states were, WestBengal > Uttar Pradesh > Kerala > Bihar. As of update in January 2022, some of the densely populated states in India include: Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, West Bengal, Maharashtra, Kerala. Population density is typically measured by dividing the total population of an area by its land area. The state with the highest population density in India is Bihar. Bihar with 1,106 population persons per sq . km is most thickly populated state followed by West Bengal (1,028)and Kerala (860). The Indian state with the lowest population density is Arunachal Pradesh. It is known as the “Land of the dawn-lit mountains”. Delhi is the Union Territory having the highest density of population, and the Union Territory with the lowest density of population is Andaman & Nicobar Islands.Macao in China is the most densely populated country in the world as well as Asia.

Baljek Airport, also known as Tura Airport, is one of the smallest airports in India. It is located in Meghalaya, about 33 kilometers northeast of Tura.

question: If instead of wooden ploughs iron ploughs are used it would be an example of............ (UPSC Prelims 1983)
option_1: Obsolete technology
option_2: Appropriate technology
option_3: Redundant technology
option_4: Ultimate technology
solution_text:
EXPLANATION : (B) 

The term “appropriate technology” refers to a form of technology that is suitable, affordable, and sustainable for a particular region or community, especially in developing or rural areas. It balances efficiency, cost, and local resource use, aiming to improve productivity without causing social or environmental disruption. In this case, replacing wooden ploughs with iron ploughs represents a technological improvement that enhances agricultural efficiency and durability while still being affordable and adaptable for rural farmers. The change does not require highly advanced machinery or costly imports, making it a practical and context-appropriate innovation.

question:

option_1: 1, 2, 3, 4

solution_text: EXPLANATION : (C) The Fifth Five-Year Plan in India covered the period from 1974 to 1979 and focused on achieving specific economic and social objectives. 1) The plan aimed to reduce poverty through targeted programs andpolicies. Employment generation and income redistribution were emphasized to uplift the economically weaker sections of society. 2) Economic Self Sufficiency 3) Industrial growth and diversification were significant goals. question: North-South dialogue refers to talks............. (UPSC Prelims 1983)
option_1: For reorganisation of States between North India and South India
option_2: For sharing economic resources between rich countries of the North and poor countries of the South
option_3: Between countries of North America and South America for economic cooperation.
option_4: Between USA and USSR in the field of space research
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (B) The North-South Dialogue is a multilateral discussion about the international economic system that began in the 1970s. India took the lead in South-South cooperation after the North-South Dialogue failed. The North-South Dialogue is a negotiation process between developing countries and industrialized countries. The developing countries are predominantly located in Asia, Africa, and Latin America. The industrialized countries are located in North America and Western Europe. The North-South dialogue typically refers to discussions and negotiations between developed (North) and developing (South) countries, addressing global economic, social, and environmental issues. It often involves matters like trade, aid, and sustainable development.

question: The tendency of lower caste people to improve their living standard and status by imitating the upper class people is known as............ (UPSC Prelims 1983)

question: In a civilised urban society, what makes people live together? (UPSC Prelims 1983)

question: In India the computation of national income is often difficult because of............ (UPSC Prelims 1983)

question: The maximum revenue source of village panchayats is........... (UPSC Prelims 1983)

question: New high yielding varieties of food crops are different from the conventional in that they need.......... (UPSC Prelims 1984)
option_1: Less water
option_2: Less fertilisers
option_3: More water and more fertilisers
option_4: Less water and more fertilizers
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (D) New high-yielding varieties of food crops are different from conventional varieties in that they often require specificand optimized conditions for optimal performance. These conditions may include precise levels of fertilizers, water, and environmental factors. What is High Yielding Variety? HYV crops are varieties that have been selectively bred to produce higher yields compared to traditional varieties. These crops are often developed to be more resistant to pests and diseases, have better adaptation to specific environmental conditions, and generally exhibit improved characteristics that result in increased productivity. High-Yielding Variety (HYV) seeds were first introduced in Indian agriculture in 1965. The Green Revolution, which began in 1965, combined the introduction of HYV seeds with better irrigation and fertilizer use. Most important high yield variety seeds can be found in wheat, corn, soybean, rice, potato, and cotton.

question: Mica is abundantly used in which of the following industries? (UPSC Prelims 1984)
option_1: Cement
option_2: Electrical
option_3: Plastic
option_4: Fire resistance bricks
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (B) Mica is known for its excellent heat resistance, electrical insulation properties,and transparency. Commonly found in igneous and metamorphic rocks, mica is often split into thin sheets, forming layers that can be easily peeled. India is a major producer of mica, which is found around the northern edge of the Chota Nagpur plateau. The main region for mica production is Jharkhand’s Koderma Gaya-Hazaribagh belt. Most of Rajasthan’s mica is produced near Ajmer. Mica is a non-metallic mineral. It’s a transparent mineral found in igneous rocks that’s made up of a series of plates or leaves. Mica is shiny and non-conductive of electricity and heat. India is the world’s largest producer and exporter of mica. In 2022, India’s mica exports were valued at around $648 million.

question: Stagflation refers to......... (UPSC Prelims 1984)
option_1: Constant rate of inflation
option_2: Low inflation with high recession
option_3: High inflation with low recession
option_4: Stagnation and inflation
solution_text: EXPLANATION : (D) Stagflation, in simple terms, occurs when an economy experiences a combination of slow economic growth (stagnation), high unemployment, and rising prices (inflation) at the same time. This creates a challenging situation for policymakers because the usual tools to address one issue may worsen the other.
